name: rex-vscode
description: Local AI runtime client for VS Code and Cursor, backed by the REX daemon.

commands.title: REX: How to Start the Daemon

rex.cliPath.description: Executable path or name for the REX CLI (status and complete subcommands). Defaults to lookup on PATH.

rex.daemonBinaryPath.description: Executable path or name for the REX binary when auto-starting the daemon (rex daemon). Used only when auto-start is enabled.

rex.daemonAutoStart.description: Spawn rex daemon automatically if it is not running. Disabled by default.

rex.modelId.description: Optional model id passed to rex complete as --model on every request. Leave empty to use the daemon default.

rex.rexRoot.description: Optional REX_ROOT path passed to the daemon child when auto-start is enabled. Leave empty to use the default layout (~/.rex or $REX_ROOT from the environment).

rex.productAgentConfig.description: When true (default), merge sidecars.active=agent and agent.approvals_enabled into project .rex/config.json on auto-start. Set false for stub/harness-only workspaces.

walkthroughs.title: REX: get started (local)
walkthroughs.description: Point the extension at the **rex** CLI, run **rex daemon** with brokered HTTP and an enabled sidecar for **plan/agent** modes, then open chat. **ask** mode may use mock when the daemon is configured for harness-only paths.

steps.title: rex must be visible to the editor
steps.description:
The extension spawns **rex status** and **rex complete** with the **extension host** environment, which may have a smaller `PATH` than your terminal. If the status bar shows **REX unavailable** while the same command works in a terminal, set **REX: Cli Path** to the **absolute** path to `rex`.

[Open the REX output channel](command:rex.openOutput)

steps.title: Start rex daemon (or enable auto-start)
steps.description:
**User-managed (default):** run `rex daemon` in a terminal so it listens on `/tmp/rex.sock`.

**Extension-managed:** set **rex.daemonAutoStart** to `true` and, if needed, set **REX: Daemon Binary Path** to the absolute `rex` binary.

[How to start the daemon](command:rex.howToStartDaemon)
[Show daemon status](command:rex.showStatus)

steps.title: Configure brokered HTTP and sidecar (plan/agent)
steps.description:
Run `rex config init`, then edit `$REX_ROOT/config.json` or project `.rex/config.json`:

- **Sidecar:** `sidecars.active`: `agent`, list entry with `binary`: `rex-agent` (install: `pip install -e sidecars/rex-agent`, `rex proto install`).
- **HTTP inference:** `inference.runtime`: `http-openai-compat`, `inference.openai_compat.base_url` (example Ollama `http://127.0.0.1:11434/v1`), `model`: e.g. `llama3.2`.

Run `rex config validate` and `rex sidecar doctor`. Details: **docs/EXTENSION_LOCAL_E2E.md** §3 and **docs/CONFIGURATION.md** in the REX repo.

[How to start the daemon](command:rex.howToStartDaemon)

steps.title: Open REX chat and send a prompt
steps.description:
When the status bar is **REX ready**, open chat beside the editor (secondary sidebar on recent VS Code hosts) or from the activity bar on older hosts. Use **REX: Open Chat in Editor** for a full tab. Shortcuts: focus chat (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+L), stop generation (Escape while streaming), clear chat (Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+K).

[REX: Open Chat](command:rex.focusChat)

scripts.build: npm run build:extension && npm run build:webview
scripts.package: npm run build && npm run package:vsix
